title: MultisensoryFlickerHealthyYoungAdults_AllSubjectsRawData
description: This dataset comprises scalp electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from healthy young adults exposed to audiovisual flicker stimulation at 40 Hz, control (no flicker), or randomized flicker conditions during a one-hour psychomotor vigilance task. The study investigates the effects of multisensory flicker on neural activity and cognitive performance, providing raw EEG data suitable for analysis of frequency-domain responses and task-related brain dynamics.
methods_description: Scalp EEG activity was recorded from healthy adults during one-hour sessions of either 40 Hz audiovisual flicker stimulation, no flicker control condition, or randomized flicker as sham stimulation. Subjects performed a psychomotor vigilance task throughout the recording session.
